content.problemStatement:
You are given 222 binary strings SSS and TTT, of length NNN. Here, binary strings means both SSS and TTT contain only 000s and 111s.

Is it possible to make SSS equal to TTT using at most 3‚čÖN3 \cdot N3‚čÖN such operations:


Choose 1‚Č§i<N1 \le i < N1‚Č§i<N such that Si=1S_i = 1Si‚Äč=1 and then flip Si+1S_{i + 1}Si+1‚Äč, i.e. change it from 000 to 111 or 111 to 000.
If it is possible, please print an operation sequence. If it is impossible, print ‚ąí1-1‚ąí1. You do not need to minimize the length of the sequence. If multiple sequences are possible, all will be accepted.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line contains NNN - the length of the string.
The second line contains SSS - the initial string.
The third line contains TTT - the target string.




Output Format
For each test case, print the following:



if it is possible, print:

on the first line, print K(0‚Č§K‚Č§3‚čÖN)K (0 \le K \le 3 \cdot N)K(0‚Č§K‚Č§3‚čÖN) the number of operations performed.
on the second line, print KKK integers X1,X2,...,XK(1‚Č§Xi<N)X_1, X_2, ..., X_K (1 \le X_i < N)X1‚Äč,X2‚Äč,...,XK‚Äč(1‚Č§Xi‚Äč<N) the indices chosen for each of the KKK operations in that order.


if it is not possible, print ‚ąí1-1‚ąí1.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2‚čÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚čÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : In the first test case, we first choose i=3i = 3i=3 (note S3=1S_3 = 1S3‚Äč=1) and flip S4S_4S4‚Äč to get 011101110111. Then, we choose i=2i = 2i=2 (note S2=1S_2 = 1S2‚Äč=1) and flip S3S_3S3‚Äč to get 010101010101, which equals TTT.
Test Case 2 : It can be proven to be impossible.
